```{python}

import os
import pandas as pd 
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pygwalker as pyg
import seaborn as sns

# Load the Parkinson's dataset
df = pd.read_csv(r'parkinsons_updrs.data')
df.tail(10)

# Set seaborn theme for consistent styling across all plots
# pallette="pastel" = soft, muted colors
sns.set_theme(style="white", palette="pastel")

# Print all column names to verify data structure
verify_column = df.columns
print(verify_column)

# Convert sex column from numeric to text labels
# 0 = Male, 1 = Female
df['sex'].astype('str')
df['sex'] = df['sex'].replace({0: 'Male', 1: 'Female'})

# Create horizontal boxplots showing UPDRS score
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
sns.boxplot(data=df, x="total_UPDRS", y='sex', palette=["m", "g"])
sns.lineplot()

# Add labels and styling
plt.title('Distribuition of Parkinson by Gender', size='15')
plt.xlabel('Gender', size='12')
plt.ylabel('Number of Pacients', size='12')
sns.despine(offset=10, trim=True)
plt.show()

```


```{python}

# Filter dataset so only include patients younger than 70
df = df[df['age'] < 70]

# Ensure sex column is properly labeled
df['sex'] = df['sex'].replace({0: 'Male', 1: 'Female'})

# Calculate average motor UPDRS score for each gender
avg_by_gender = df.groupby('sex')['motor_UPDRS'].mean().reset_index()

# Create horizontal bar chart
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4))
sns.barplot(
    data=avg_by_gender,
    x='motor_UPDRS',
    y='sex',
    palette=['#4A90E2', '#E94B77'],
    alpha=0.8
)

# Display legends of graphic: Title, XLabel and YLabel
plt.title('Average Motor UPDRS Score by Gender (Age < 70)', fontsize=13, pad=15)
plt.xlabel('Average Motor UPDRS Score', fontsize=11)
plt.ylabel('Gender', fontsize=11)

# Add numeric labels on each bar showing the exact value
for i, row in avg_by_gender.iterrows():
    plt.text(row['motor_UPDRS'], i, f" {row['motor_UPDRS']:.1f}",
            va='center', fontsize=10)

sns.despine()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('gender_barplot.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()

```

```{python}

# Convert sex to string and replace values
df['sex'] = df['sex'].astype('str').replace({0: 'Male', 1: 'Female'})

# Create scatter plot with additional dimensions (size, colors)
g = sns.relplot(
    data=df,
    x='Jitter(%)', 
    y="total_UPDRS",
    hue="sex", 
    size='age',
    sizes=(30, 200),
    alpha=0.5,
    palette=['#4A90E2', '#E94B77'],
    height=6,
    aspect=1.5
)

# Add title above the plot
g.fig.suptitle('Voice Jitter vs Disease Severity', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', y=1.02)

# Set axis labels
g.set_axis_labels("Jitter(%)", "Total UPDRS Score", fontsize=12)

# Calculate and display correlation statistics
from scipy import stats # Statistical functions
corr, p_value = stats.pearsonr(df['Jitter(%)'], df['total_UPDRS'])

# Add text box with correlation into to the plot
g.ax.text(0.05, 0.95, 
          f'Overall r = {corr:.3f}\np < {p_value:.3f}',
          transform = g.ax.transAxes,
          fontsize=10,
          verticalalignment='top',
          bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='white', alpha=0.8))
          
# Add grid for easier reading
g.ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, linestyle='--')
g.ax.set_axisbelow(True)

sns.despine()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('relplot_improved.png', dpi=150) # dpi --> Is the resolution in dots per inch. 
plt.show()


```

```{python}

# Change theme to "darkgrid"
sns.set_theme(style="darkgrid")

# Reload dataset fresh (good practice for independent chunks)
df3 = pd.read_csv(r'parkinsons_updrs.data')

# Display columns names
df3.columns

# Filter for patients under 70
df3 = df3[df3['age'] < 70]

# Convert sex to text labels
df3['sex'] = df3['sex'].astype('str').replace({0: 'Male', 1: 'Female'})

# Rename "subject#" column to "number#" (# in column names can be problematic)
df3 = df3.rename(columns={'subject#': 'number#'})

# Create joint plot: scatter + marginal histogram
g = sns.jointplot(
              x='age', 
              y='number#', 
              data=df3, 
              kind='reg',                # regression line
              color='#6A5ACD',         # nice purple color
              height=8,                  # larger plots
              space=0.2,                 # space between joint and margina plots
              marginal_kws=dict(bins=20, fill=True, alpha=0.6), # better histograms
              joint_kws=dict(scatter_kws={'alpha': 0.5, 's': 30}) # semi-transparent points
              )

# Add title(positioned above the plot)
g.fig.suptitle('Relationship Between Age and Subject Number\n (Patients Age > 70)', y=0.98, fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')

# Set axis labels
g.set_axis_labels('Age (years)', 'Subject Number', fontsize=12)

# Calculate correlation between age and subject number
from scipy import stats
corr, p_value = stats.pearsonr(df3['age'], df3['number#'])

# Add correlation info as text box on the joint plot
g.ax_joint.text(
    0.05, 0.95, 
    f'r = {corr:.3f}\np = {p_value:.3f}',
    transform=g.ax_joint.transAxes,
    fontsize=11,
    verticalalignment='top',
    bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='white', alpha=0.8)
)

# tight_layout --> automatically adjusts subplot parameters to prevent overlaps
plt.tight_layout() 

# Save figure with high resolution
plt.savefig('gender_hexbin.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')

# Show the plot
plt.show()


```

```{python}

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Set theme with "ticks"
sns.set_theme(style="ticks")

# Load data fresh
df = pd.read_csv(r'parkinsons_updrs.data')

# Convert sex to labels
df['sex'] = df['sex'].replace({0: 'Male', 1: 'Female'})

# Select only keys columns for pairplot to keep it manageable
# Pairplot creates NxN grid of plots, so fewer columns = faster rendering
key_cols = ['age', 'sex', 'motor_UPDRS', 'total_UPDRS']

# Create pairplot: shows relationship between all pairs of variables
sns.pairplot(df[key_cols], hue='sex', palette=['#4A90E2', '#E94B77'])

# Save the complete figure
plt.savefig('pairplot_key_features.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()

```
